food_name: Kuih Semperit (Malay Butter Flower Cookie with Glacé Cherry)
food_subgroup: Butter shortbread-style cookies; piped flower-shaped cornflour-dominant butter cookie topped with glacé cherry

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no concept for Kuih Semperit. Closest parent is butter shortbread cookie (FOODON:03301710). No exact FoodOn URI available for this traditional Malay cornflour-butter cookie.
ontology.foodex2_description: Biscuits and cookies — closest FoodEx2 parent category for butter-based shortbread-style cookies. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for Kuih Semperit; A039V (Biscuits and cookies) used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for Kuih Semperit. A039V (Biscuits and cookies) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Kuih Semperit or equivalent traditional Malay butter cookie.

preparation_method: piped through cookie press or semperit tube

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Kuih Semperit is 3 pieces, weighing approximately 27g in total (each flower-shaped piece approximately 9g), as commonly served on a Hari Raya open-house kuih tray.

nutrient_source.title: Kuih Semperit — IMR Malaysia MyFCD (Malaysian Food Composition Database) cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo database for butter shortbread-type cookies
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research Malaysia (MyFCD) and Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from IMR Malaysia MyFCD entry for Kuih Semperit cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo database for comparable butter shortbread-type cookies. Values represent a cornflour-dominant (approximately 60% cornflour) butter cookie formulation. Energy 460 kcal/100g, fat 24g/100g (saturated 14g), carbohydrate 58g/100g, protein 5g/100g are consistent with published values for this cookie type.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index estimation for cornflour-dominant butter cookie — analogous to shortbread cookie GI data from University of Sydney GI Database; fat-moderated GI estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate basis)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kuih Semperit in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at approximately 45 (low-medium) based on the following analogous reasoning: cornflour has a moderate GI (~65), but the dominant butter fat content (24g/100g) substantially moderates glycemic absorption. Closest analogous food in the University of Sydney GI Database is shortbread cookie (GI ~40–55). Glycemic load per 27g serving = (45 × 15.7g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 7.1 (low GL). Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Saturated fat (14g/100g) and moderate sodium (100mg/100g); cardiovascular risk from high saturated fat intake relevant to hypertension-hyperlipidaemia comorbidity
hypertension.guidance: A single 27g serving contributes approximately 27mg sodium (low), but the high saturated fat content (3.8g per serving) is a secondary cardiovascular concern for patients with concurrent hypertension and hyperlipidaemia; limit to 1–2 servings and avoid repeated consumption at open-house events.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low-medium GI (~45) and low glycemic load per 3-piece serving (~7.1), but high caloric density (460 kcal/100g) and social over-eating risk at Hari Raya gatherings
type2_diabetes.guidance: A 3-piece (27g) serving has a manageable glycemic load of approximately 7.1; however, the small individual piece size (9g each) makes portion control difficult in open-house settings, and consuming 10 pieces (90g) raises the glycemic load to approximately 26 (high) — patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to 3 pieces per occasion and monitor cumulative intake across multiple kuih types.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat content (14g/100g from butter) and cholesterol (80mg/100g); a 27g serving delivers 3.8g saturated fat, representing approximately 19% of the recommended daily saturated fat limit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high saturated fat content (14g/100g) from butter makes Kuih Semperit a high-concern food for cardiovascular disease patients; limit to 1–2 pieces per occasion and avoid regular consumption, particularly when combined with other high-saturated-fat Hari Raya kuih.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kuih Semperit (also spelled Semprit or Kueh Semperit) are delicate, flower-shaped Malay butter cookies that are a quintessential Hari Raya Aidilfitri open-house staple in Singapore and Malaysia, served alongside Kuih Makmur and Kuih Cornflake on festive kuih trays. The characteristic melt-in-mouth crumbliness comes from a cornflour-dominant base (approximately 60% cornflour) combined with a generous amount of butter, creating a texture so tender the cookie begins to dissolve on the tongue before fully biting. The name 'semperit' or 'semprit' refers to the piping technique — the dough is extruded through a cookie press or semperit tube to form the distinctive flower shape. Each piece is topped with a red glacé cherry or almond before baking. At 460 kcal/100g with 24g fat/100g (of which 14g is saturated fat from butter) and 58g carbohydrate/100g, Kuih Semperit is calorie-dense relative to its small size (~9g per piece). The estimated GI of approximately 45 (low-medium) is lower than expected for a cornflour-based product because the dominant butter fat moderates glycemic absorption. The glycemic load per standard 3-piece (27g) serving is approximately 7.1 (low). The primary clinical counselling concern mirrors Kuih Makmur: the per-piece caloric and glycemic impact is manageable, but the social context of eating from a shared open-house tray drives over-consumption. Kuih Semperit is vegetarian and halal. It contains gluten (plain flour), dairy (butter), and egg (egg yolk) allergens.
common_user_queries: how many calories in kuih semperit
is kuih semperit suitable for diabetics

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from IMR Malaysia MyFCD cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo database for this specific traditional Malay butter cookie. Values are internally consistent (energy check: 5g protein × 4 + 24g fat × 9 + 58g carbohydrate × 4 = 20 + 216 + 232 = 468 kcal, consistent with reported 460 kcal within rounding tolerance). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database shortbread cookie data with documented reasoning.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for Kuih Semperit is not available in IMR Malaysia MyFCD or HPB Singapore Foodo database as of 2026-04-13. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this baked cookie not reported in available approved sources. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per source data — no partially hydrogenated oils used in traditional formulation). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — this is a baked butter cookie with no vitamin C-containing ingredients). GI value of 45 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database shortbread cookie data; no direct GI measurement for Kuih Semperit exists in approved sources. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (45) × available carbohydrate per serving (15.66g from 58g/100g × 27g) / 100 = 7.05, rounded to 7.1.
